question: Will "Thrash" be the top US Netflix movie this week?

fragility_points: The view would weaken sharply if Thrash had verified release timing and strong daily US ranking during 4/6-4/12.
key_assumptions: Titles usually need either incumbent momentum or a verified major release pulse to take weekly Netflix #1.
Absence from the currently published US top 10 is meaningful negative baseline evidence for Thrash.
main_logical_chain: Read the authoritative Netflix US Top 10 movies page and confirm the currently published week is 3/30/26-4/5/26.
Observe that Anaconda is #1 and Thrash is absent from the published US top 10.
Note that the market settles on the next 4/14 publication for 4/6-4/12, so prior published chart status is baseline context rather than final settlement.
Apply outside-view logic: without direct evidence of a major Thrash release surge, a 90% Yes price is too high.
payload.main_thesis: The market is overconfident: absent direct evidence that Thrash had a major US release surge in the 4/6-4/12 window, it is much less likely than 90% to finish #1 on the next Netflix US movie update.
quote_anchors: Anaconda #1 on current published US week 3/30/26-4/5/26
Thrash not present in published US top 10
payload.source_quality_view: High-quality source-of-truth for settlement mechanics and current published chart status; weaker for predicting the next unpublished week without additional title-specific evidence.
strongest_disconfirmers: Thrash could still be a fresh release that surged during the unresolved 4/6-4/12 window and wins the 4/14 update.
strongest_supports: Netflix's own US movies page still showed 3/30/26-4/5/26 with Anaconda at #1 on 2026-04-13.
Thrash was not present in the currently published US top 10.
The contract names Netflix's Top 10 page as the source of truth and the next update is not yet published.
payload.timing_relevance: High: the current page precedes the 2026-04-14 update and the market depends on the exact 4/6/26-4/12/26 reporting window in ET.
unresolved_ambiguities: Thrash release timing and traction in the covered week were not directly verified from authoritative title-specific surfaces in this run.
payload.what_would_change_view: Direct proof that Thrash released in-window and dominated US Netflix viewing, or the 4/14 Netflix update itself showing Thrash at/near #1.

fragility_points: A single chart update can instantly invalidate all pre-publication inference.
Evidence independence is limited because both substantive sources are first-party Netflix surfaces.
key_assumptions: The market's strong lean toward Thrash reflects some real signal rather than pure momentum.
Netflix will publish the relevant 4/6/26-4/12/26 US movie chart on roughly normal Tuesday cadence.
No alternative film will surprise to the upside once the unpublished chart appears.
main_logical_chain: The contract resolves from the Netflix Tudum US movie chart update for 4/6/26-4/12/26.
That chart was not yet published on 2026-04-13, so the highest-information catalyst remained pending.
With no direct chart proof yet, a high-but-sub-90% estimate is more defensible than simply matching the market's near-certainty.
payload.main_thesis: Thrash is still the likeliest winner, but the decisive catalyst is the pending Netflix US Top 10 chart update, so confidence should sit below the market's near-certainty until the chart is published.
quote_anchors: Anaconda at No. 1 with 9.9 million views
payload.source_quality_view: Source-of-truth quality is high because Netflix Tudum is named in the contract, but forecasting confirmation before publication is still limited.
strongest_disconfirmers: There was no direct authoritative chart evidence yet that Thrash was No. 1 for 4/6/26-4/12/26 when checked.
The last published week on the governing page was still 3/30/26-4/5/26, led by Anaconda.
strongest_supports: Netflix Tudum is the named source of truth and confirms the key catalyst is still ahead.
The market is already heavily concentrated on Thrash versus alternatives.
First-party contextual Thrash mention on April 10 is weakly consistent with current audience traction.
payload.timing_relevance: This is primarily a timing market: the Tuesday April 14 Netflix chart publication is the dominant repricing event.
unresolved_ambiguities: What pre-publication information the market is aggregating in support of Thrash is not directly observable here.
No authoritative public surface checked here revealed the unpublished week winner before the scheduled update.
payload.what_would_change_view: I would change quickly if Netflix posts the 4/6/26-4/12/26 chart with another film at No. 1, if a more authoritative pre-release data surface shows a different leader, or if update timing/mechanics differ from the stated ET schedule.

fragility_points: This view relies on trusting market aggregation more than directly visible public evidence.
A single official update can fully overturn the estimate.
Independent pre-publication evidence for Thrash was sparse.
key_assumptions: The market is pricing a real unseen lead for Thrash in the 4/6/26-4/12/26 reporting window.
Netflix will publish the expected Tuesday U.S. Top 10 update on schedule.
Thrash was eligible and fully counted in the relevant U.S. weekly window.
main_logical_chain: Start from the 0.90 market price as an information-rich prior.
Verify the governing source-of-truth surface and exact date/timing mechanics.
Observe that Netflix had not yet posted the relevant target-week chart, so the market was pricing an expected result rather than a visible settled one.
Keep Thrash as the favorite because the market may still be aggregating real off-page information, but cut the estimate below market because direct public evidence does not justify near-certainty.
payload.main_thesis: Thrash is still the most likely winner, but the 90% market price looks too confident because Netflix had not yet published the target-week U.S. chart when checked.
payload.source_quality_view: High-quality governing source for mechanics and current publication state, but limited independent evidence on the unseen target-week result itself.
strongest_disconfirmers: The authoritative Netflix U.S. films page still showed 3/30/26-4/5/26, not the target week, when checked.
There was no directly published authoritative evidence yet showing Thrash at #1.
The currently published official chart had Anaconda at #1, underscoring that the market was extrapolating ahead of publication.
strongest_supports: The market is a low-complexity chart market with a clear governing source and imminent publication.
A 90% price suggests traders believe Thrash already won the unseen target week rather than merely being a slight favorite.
Nothing found suggested elevated risk that Netflix would fail to publish the update.
payload.timing_relevance: Highly timing-sensitive: on 2026-04-13 around 11:25 ET, the governing Netflix page still had not rolled to the 4/6/26-4/12/26 week, while the market closed that evening and expected publication was the next day at 3:00 PM ET.
unresolved_ambiguities: What specific evidence traders were using to justify the extreme price.
Whether Thrash had a dominant underlying U.S. weekly lead or merely a market-narrative lead before publication.
payload.what_would_change_view: I would move lower if Netflix publishes the target-week chart with another film at #1 or if evidence shows Thrash was not eligible; I would move closer to market if a direct Netflix-associated or strongly independent source tied Thrash to a dominant target-week performance.

fragility_points: Exact-title mapping risk between market label and Netflix chart label.
Pre-publication timing risk because settlement depends on a not-yet-posted weekly chart.
Extreme market confidence built on thin direct public confirmation.
key_assumptions: 'Thrash' cleanly maps to the exact title Netflix will use on the eventual US Top 10 chart.
Market confidence reflects real off-platform information rather than title-mapping error.
No alternative listed film overtakes it once the 4/6/26-4/12/26 chart posts.
main_logical_chain: Start from the market view that Thrash is the clear favorite.
Check the governing Netflix source and confirm the relevant week is not yet published.
Observe that the latest published US chart shows Anaconda #1 and no visible Thrash support.
Discount the market for unpublished-window, title-mapping, and operational risk.
Conclude Thrash can still be favorite, but not near-lock favorite.
payload.main_thesis: Thrash may still be the likeliest single outcome, but the market is too confident because the governing Netflix chart for the relevant week was still unpublished and the latest published US chart showed a different #1.
payload.source_quality_view: Primary source quality is high because Netflix Tudum is the governing chart surface; contextual market pricing is useful but not direct evidence of resolution.
strongest_disconfirmers: Netflix's own US Top 10 page checked on 2026-04-13 still showed 3/30/26-4/5/26 with Anaconda at #1, not Thrash.
Thrash was not visible on the published US top 10 checked during the run.
The market resolves before the relevant Netflix update is published, creating timing and mapping risk.
strongest_supports: Polymarket pricing heavily favored Thrash at roughly 90%-95%.
This is an official-chart market with a clear governing source, so a single dominant favorite is plausible.
payload.timing_relevance: High: market closes 2026-04-13 20:00 ET, while the Netflix update is expected 2026-04-14 15:00 ET for the 4/6/26-4/12/26 window.
unresolved_ambiguities: Whether Netflix will print the relevant winning title exactly as 'Thrash'.
Whether traders have valid off-platform information not visible on the public chart yet.
payload.what_would_change_view: I would move up if Netflix publishes the relevant US chart with Thrash clearly #1 or confirms the exact title mapping; I would move down if a different film tops the chart or Thrash never appears as the chart label.

fragility_points: A credible direct tracking source could justify moving back toward the market.

key_assumptions: The market's Thrash consensus reflects informative but unofficial pre-update signals.
Netflix's next published US movie chart will be the governing settlement surface for the week ending 2026-04-12.
main_logical_chain: Settlement is governed by Netflix's US weekly movie chart.
At research time, Netflix still displayed the prior published week rather than the decisive week ending 2026-04-12.
That means the final answer is not yet directly published on the authoritative surface.
So Thrash can still be the favorite, but near-certainty is not fully justified by published evidence.
payload.main_thesis: Thrash remains the likely winner, but the market is overconfident before Netflix has published the decisive US weekly chart.
quote_anchors: official Netflix surface still showed only the prior published week
the edge, if any, is against market overconfidence
payload.source_quality_view: High-quality official settlement source, but the decisive week was not yet published; contextual market source is weaker and non-settling.
strongest_disconfirmers: The market may already be correctly incorporating accurate unofficial tracking, so 90% could be fair.
There is no direct evidence from checked sources that another title led the deciding week.
strongest_supports: No checked source directly contradicted Thrash as likely #1.
Official Netflix source had not yet published the decisive week, making 90% look too certain.
payload.timing_relevance: High: the case resolves off a Netflix update expected on 2026-04-14 3 PM ET for the 2026-04-06 to 2026-04-12 reporting window.
unresolved_ambiguities: What unofficial evidence the market is using to price Thrash near certainty.
Whether any rival title had stronger weekly US view momentum than market pricing suggests.
